response.justification: Bogdan is a strong senior individual contributor with a genuine passion for developer tooling and testing — he authored test frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts), built CI/CD and device-orchestration infrastructure, and currently works on AI developer tooling. However, this is a Level II people-management role requiring at least 2 years of people management and 3 years of formal technical leadership, and his resume shows no direct reports, no team-leadership titles, and no organizational-change experience across an 18-year IC career. His mobile experience (iOS/Apple TV app maintenance) is real but dated and narrow relative to a large mobile-platform ecosystem. He would be a credible candidate for a senior/staff IC role on this team, but not for the manager position as scoped.
strengths: Deep, authentic developer-tools and test-infrastructure background: test runners, assertion libraries, CI servers, GitLab/DevOps setup, and MCP code generators
18+ years of broad engineering experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput low-latency systems
Strong teaching and mentoring instincts — created multi-day Selenium and web-internals courses, ran refactoring dojos and conference workshops
concerns: No people-management experience at all, against a hard minimum of 2 years managing or leading a team
No formal technical-leadership role (roadmap ownership, org-level priorities, performance management) evident in any position
Developer-tooling work is largely personal open-source projects rather than delivering tooling for a large engineering org, and mobile-platform depth is limited and dated
